ConfidenceScore.description:
A score for the confidence in the issue / error detection.

- The raw score is the score as output by the underlying model.
- The normalized score is rescaled in such a way that the interval between 0 and 1 maps to the defined confidence levels.
ConfidenceScore.title: ConfidenceScore

timestamp.description: Unix timestamp when message was created
timestamp.title: Timestamp

DiffToolContent.description: Content for diff-producing tools (Write, Edit, MultiEdit)
DiffToolContent.title: DiffToolContent

errorType.description: Type of error, i.e. name of the exception that was raised
errorType.title: Errortype

EvidenceMessage.description: Message containing a piece of evidence.

timestamp.description: Unix timestamp when message was created
timestamp.title: Timestamp

EvidenceMessage.title: EvidenceMessage

source.description: A file path on the users local machine.
source.title: Source

GenericToolContent.description: Generic content for most tools - just a string
GenericToolContent.title: GenericToolContent

ImbueCLIToolContent.description: Content for Imbue CLI MCP results that may be composed of multiple actions

actionOutputs.description: List of action outputs from the Imbue CLI tool
actionOutputs.title: Actionoutputs

ImbueCLIToolContent.title: ImbueCLIToolContent

IssueCode.description:
A code for the type of issue / error detected.

The code can either correspond something very specific (e.g. "ambiguous_docstring")
or to something more general (e.g. "function_implementation").

The latter case would be used as an "umbrella" code in cases we don't know what exactly comes out of an issue verifier.
IssueCode.title: IssueCode

MetadataMessage.description: Message containing metadata about the scout run.

timestamp.description: Unix timestamp when message was created
timestamp.title: Timestamp

MetadataMessage.title: MetadataMessage

ScoreMessage.description: Message containing an overall score assessment.

timestamp.description: Unix timestamp when message was created
timestamp.title: Timestamp

ScoreMessage.title: ScoreMessage

ScoutEvidenceExample.description: A single example of evidence for the report.
ScoutEvidenceExample.title: ScoutEvidenceExample

SeverityScore.description:
A score for the severity of the issue / error.

- The raw score is the score as potentially output by the underlying model.
- The normalized score is rescaled in such a way that the interval between 0 and 1 maps to the defined severity levels.
SeverityScore.title: SeverityScore

timestamp.description: Unix timestamp when message was created
timestamp.title: Timestamp

confidenceScore.description: A score between 0.0 and 1.0 indicating how confident we are that this suggestion addresses a real issue.
confidenceScore.title: Confidencescore

severityScore.description: A score between 0.0 and 1.0 indicating how severe the issue is that this suggestion addresses.
severityScore.title: Severityscore

content.description: Result content from the tool execution
content.title: Content

invocationString.description: String representation of how the tool was invoked
invocationString.title: Invocationstring

isError.description: Whether the tool execution resulted in an error
isError.title: Iserror

toolName.description: Name of the tool that was used
toolName.title: Toolname

toolUseId.description: ID of the corresponding tool use
toolUseId.title: Tooluseid

id.description: Unique identifier for this tool use
id.title: Id

name.description: Name of the tool being used
name.title: Name

warningType.description: Type of warning, i.e. name of the exception that was raised
warningType.title: Warningtype

AgentTaskStateV1.description:
The state of a run_agent server task.
This is used to snapshot the state of the task at various points in time so that the agent can be resumed.
AgentTaskStateV1.title: AgentTaskStateV1

CacheReposTaskStateV1.title: CacheReposTaskStateV1

CleanupImagesTaskStateV1.title: CleanupImagesTaskStateV1

SerializedException.title: SerializedException

AgentTaskInputsV1.description:
The primary task for running an agent.

Contains the necessary information for the task runner, i.e., the function in sculptor/tasks/handlers/run_agent/v1.py,

In particular, this defines the configuration for creating the `Environment` (e.g., the image_config, volume_config),
as well as the relevant git information (git_hash, branch_name, new_branch) for ensuring the `Environment` is set up correctly.

The `agent_config` is used to configure the `Agent` itself.  It contains the full (versioned) command to be run,
and will be injected into the `Environment` as a file that the command can then read.
AgentTaskInputsV1.title: AgentTaskInputsV1

CacheReposInputsV1.description: This is for the task which copies the user repo once a day, so that it can be baked into docker images
CacheReposInputsV1.title: CacheReposInputsV1

CleanupImagesInputsV1.description: This is for the task which cleans up excess Docker images created by deleted and archived Sculpltor tasks
CleanupImagesInputsV1.title: CleanupImagesInputsV1

MustBeShutDownTaskInputsV1.description: Used in testing to make sure we can shut down tasks that do nothing but wait.
MustBeShutDownTaskInputsV1.title: MustBeShutDownTaskInputsV1

SendEmailTaskInputsV1.description: This is mostly here so that it is clear how to make additional server task inputs types in the future
SendEmailTaskInputsV1.title: SendEmailTaskInputsV1
